steps.title: Identify what the question asks for
steps.content: The question requires us to find which choice specifies the characteristics of the test site—that is, the physical and environmental features of where the study took place. Look in the notes for descriptions of the location, setting, and climate.

steps.title: Match to the test site characteristics
steps.content:
Choice B directly describes where the test happened: the United States, in a mesic grassland with a subtropic climate and year-round rain.

These details directly answer what the test site was like. The other three focus on findings (A), the study topic (C), or what was measured (D)—none describe the test site itself.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
